dc_subject: Book thefts|Interviews|Manuscripts|Private investigators|Cornell, Raymond|Trials|Blumberg, Stephen Carrie
dc_title: Transcript of telephone interview with Raymond Cornell, conducted by Nicholas A. Basbanes, 30 June 1992.
dc_rights: In copyright - Educational Use Permitted; For more information see:  http://rightsstatements.org/vocab/InC-EDU/1.0/
dc_description: From the professional archives of Nicholas A. Basbanes, now held by Cushing Memorial Library & Archives, Texas A&M University.
dcterms_abstract: Transcript of a telephone interview with Raymond Cornell, a private investigator hired by Stephen Blumberg's father to help prepare the defense for his trial in 1991.  The interview was conducted by Nicholas A. Basbanes, 30 June 1992.  At the time of the trial, Blumberg was accused of stealing more than 23,600 rare, valuable and assorted other books from 268 or more universities and museums in 45 states, two Canadian provinces, and Washington, D.C.
